rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly as a key theoretical contrast in the citing paper's framing of cross-stimulus sensitization. The authors position Wadhwa et al. (2008) as representing a general reward-drive account (broad hedonic seeking after sampling) and then design multiple studies to show a more selective complement-based mechanism instead. They return to this contrast in hypotheses, experiment discussions, and the general discussion, so the citation plays a meaningful role in argument development rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper qualifies the scope and mechanism implied by Wadhwa et al. by arguing that post-consumption sensitization is selective to perceived complements, not a broad increase in reward seeking across foods. It reframes prior reward-appetite evidence as a different, more general drive process and advances a narrower goal-activation account.
